title: contingency-dsl Foundations — Shared Types
description: Paradigm-neutral shared type definitions. Operant, respondent, composed, and experiment layer schemas $ref into this file for Identifier, Number, Duration, Probability, TimeUnit, and related types. Keeping these centralized guarantees uniform validation across paradigms.

Identifier.description: Lowercase-initial identifier. Must begin with a lowercase ASCII letter or underscore. Guarantees lexical disjointness from all DSL keywords (uppercase-initial) and paradigm-specific schedule / primitive names.

UpperIdentifier.description: Uppercase-initial identifier. Used for phase names in the experiment layer and for respondent extension-primitive names.

Number.description: Numeric literal (integer or decimal). Includes negative values for contexts that permit them; paradigm-level schemas add sign constraints where appropriate (e.g., operant atomic value minimum=0, exclusiveMinimum=0).

PositiveNumber.description: Strictly positive number. Used for durations, rates, and any quantity that must be > 0.

NonNegativeNumber.description: Zero or positive number. Used for contingency probabilities, delays that may be zero, and optional offsets.

PositiveInteger.description: Strictly positive integer. Used for trial counts, session counts, component indices (1-based), ratio requirements, and Repeat counts.

TimeUnit.description: Canonical time-unit enum for the AST. Source text may use 's' or 'sec' for seconds; the AST normalizes to 's'. The 'sec' alias is accepted in source but does not appear in the AST.

Duration.description: Time-valued quantity with an explicit unit. Stored as (value, unit) in the AST so that downstream tools can render the original unit without recomputation.

Probability.description: Probability in [0, 1]. Used by respondent contingency primitives (Rescorla 1967 axis convention) and by any extension that parameterizes stochastic contingencies.

Boolean.description: Boolean literal. Source form is 'true' or 'false' (lowercase).

StringLiteral.description: Source-level double-quoted string. Used for annotation values, stimulus labels, method names, and free-form metadata.

AnnotationValue.description: The value side of an annotation key=value pair. Supports scalars (string, number), time values (stored as number after normalization), and structured values (arrays and objects).
